nodes.title: Limits and Continuity
nodes.rationale: Understanding limits is fundamental to grasping the concepts of derivatives and integrals, forming the bedrock of calculus. Continuity is a direct application of limits.

nodes.title: Introduction to Derivatives
nodes.rationale: Derivatives are essential for understanding rates of change, slopes of curves, and optimization problems, which are core applications of calculus.

nodes.title: Applications of Derivatives
nodes.rationale: This module demonstrates the practical power of derivatives in solving real-world problems related to optimization, motion, and curve sketching, enhancing general curiosity.
suggested_micro_topics: Curve Sketching (First and Second Derivative Tests)

nodes.title: Introduction to Integrals
nodes.rationale: Integrals are the inverse of derivatives and are crucial for calculating areas, volumes, and accumulated change, providing another fundamental pillar of calculus.

nodes.title: Applications of Integrals
nodes.rationale: This module showcases the versatility of integrals in solving problems involving areas between curves, volumes of solids, and other real-world accumulation scenarios.

nodes.title: Sequences and Series
nodes.rationale: This topic extends calculus to infinite sums, which is vital for understanding Taylor series, Fourier series, and many advanced mathematical and scientific applications, satisfying general curiosity.

      ]
    },
    {

        "derivatives"

    },

    },

      ],

        "Sequences and Convergence",

        "Integral Test",
        "Comparison Tests",
        "Ratio and Root Tests",
        "Alternating Series Test",
        "Power Series",
        "Taylor and Maclaurin Series"
      ]
    }
  ]
}
